response: The sky goes charcoal-gray in an instant, and a jagged ribbon of lightning splits the clouds, illuminating the world in a harsh white flash. A deep, rolling boom follows, so powerful you feel it thrum in your chest like a distant drum. Cool, rain-laden wind lashes your skin, carrying the earthy scent of wet soil and the sharp, metallic tang that often precedes a downpour. As the first heavy drops spatter the ground, you can almost taste the humidity in the air while the steady hiss of rain drowns out every other sound.
